name: EuroHPC Joint Undertaking
developed_what: Procures and co-funds European quantum computers integrated with the EuroHPC supercomputing ecosystem.
related_infrastructures: JADE; Ruby; Lucy; Piast-Q; VLQ; Euro-Q-Exa; SOL; EuroQCS-Spain
notes: Strategic infrastructure/program actor rather than a hardware supplier.

name: Quantum Technologies Flagship
developed_what: Supports European projects in quantum computing, simulation, communication, sensing/metrology, basic science and education/cooperation.
notes: Mapped at Brussels as EU programme reference point, not as a single laboratory.

name: National Quantum Science and Technology Institute
developed_what: Italian PNRR/NextGenerationEU consortium for quantum science and technology research, education, outreach and knowledge transfer.
notes: Address listed by NQSTI: Piazzale Aldo Moro 7, Rome.

name: Pasqal
developed_what: Neutral-atom quantum processors and quantum simulators; supplier for JADE, Ruby and SOL in the EuroHPC layer.
notes: Global HQ listed by Pasqal in Palaiseau, France.

name: Quandela
developed_what: Full-stack photonic quantum computers, middleware/software and photonic quantum platforms; supplier lead for Lucy.
notes: Headquarter listed by Quandela at 7 Rue Léonard de Vinci, 91300 Massy.

name: attocube systems AG
developed_what: High-precision cryogenic systems and nanoscale instrumentation; consortium partner/supplier for Lucy.
notes: EuroHPC lists attocube with Quandela as supplier for Lucy.

name: Alpine Quantum Technologies
developed_what: General-purpose quantum computers based on trapped ions; supplier for Piast-Q.
notes: AQT lists Innsbruck as home of ion-trap quantum computing.

name: IQM Quantum Computers
developed_what: Superconducting on-premise quantum computers for HPC and research; supplier for VLQ and Euro-Q-Exa.
notes: Mapped to Espoo headquarters area; official product page used for Radiance/HPC platform description.

name: Qilimanjaro Quantum Tech
developed_what: Full-stack analog quantum computers and superconducting quantum annealing systems; supplier for EuroQCS-Spain.
notes: Qilimanjaro describes itself as Barcelona-based and developing full-stack analog quantum computers.

name: Jülich Supercomputing Centre
developed_what: Hosts JADE and integrates a neutral-atom quantum simulator with the JURECA DC HPC environment.
notes: Host site listed by EuroHPC for JADE.

name: CEA / Très Grand Centre de Calcul
developed_what: Operates/hosts Ruby and Lucy at the TGCC area, coupled to the Joliot-Curie supercomputer.
notes: EuroHPC lists Ruby as managed by GENCI and operated by CEA; Lucy hosted/operated by CEA.

name: Poznań Supercomputing and Networking Center
developed_what: Hosts Piast-Q and integrates trapped-ion quantum computing with the ALTAIR supercomputer environment.
notes: Host site listed by EuroHPC for Piast-Q.

name: IT4Innovations National Supercomputing Centre
developed_what: Hosts VLQ and integrates superconducting quantum computing with the KAROLINA supercomputer environment.
notes: Host site listed by EuroHPC for VLQ.

name: Leibniz Supercomputing Centre
developed_what: Hosts Euro-Q-Exa and integrates superconducting quantum computing with SuperMUC-NG.
notes: Host site listed by EuroHPC for Euro-Q-Exa.

name: CINECA
developed_what: Hosts SOL and integrates a neutral-atom quantum simulator with Leonardo.
notes: Host site listed by EuroHPC for SOL.

name: Barcelona Supercomputing Center
developed_what: Hosts EuroQCS-Spain and integrates quantum annealing with MareNostrum 5.
notes: Host site listed by EuroHPC for EuroQCS-Spain.
